question: Assuming scientists in the famous youtube video The Thinking Machine (Artificial Intelligence in the 1960s) were interviewed the same year, what is the name of the scientist predicting the sooner thinking machines or robots? Answer using the format First name Last name

research_queries: The Thinking Machine Artificial Intelligence in the 1960s YouTube video
The Thinking Machine 1960s AI documentary scientists interviewed
scientists predictions thinking machines robots 1960s documentary
The Thinking Machine AI documentary cast interviews
1960s AI predictions thinking machines documentary scientists
working_plan: Search for 'The Thinking Machine (Artificial Intelligence in the 1960s)' YouTube video
Find information about which scientists were interviewed in this documentary
Research what each scientist predicted about thinking machines/robots timeline
Identify which scientist predicted the 'sooner' timeline for thinking machines
Format the answer as 'First name Last name'

solver.reasoning_summary: From the 1960 CBS documentary 'The Thinking Machine', Oliver Selfridge made the most optimistic timeline prediction, stating 'I am convinced that machines can and will think in our lifetime.' This represented the sooner timeline prediction compared to fellow interviewees Jerome Wiesner and Claude Shannon.

artifacts_used.excerpt:
Title: Watson

Watson While it is often difficult to precisely define the moment that a new field of endeavor springs into existence, a defining event in the emergence of AI as a formal academic discipline was certainly the Dartmouth Conference of 1956. This conference is credited with coining the term “artificial intelligence”. It was attended by a number of individuals, such as John McCarthy, Marvin Minsky, and Claude Shannon who went on to be become the ‘founding fathers’ of the field.
